description:
You are working as a capacity-planning engineer and want every new interactive
  shell on this machine to start in “planner” mode.

  Goal:
    • Add a single environment-variable definition to the end of your personal
      shell start-up file.

  Technical requirements:
    1. The line that must be appended is **exactly** (without any extra spaces):
         export CAPACITY_MODE="planner"
    2. The file you must modify is
         /home/user/.bashrc
       – If the file is missing, create it first.  
    3. Perform the modification with **one** shell command (you may use command
       chaining with “&&” or “;” if you wish, but everything has to be triggered
       by pressing <Enter> once).
    4. Immediately after running your single command, display the last line of
       /home/user/.bashrc so the grader can see the result.  
       • Acceptable verification command examples include  
         “tail -n 1 /home/user/.bashrc” or “grep -F … /home/user/.bashrc”.  
       • Whatever method you choose, the **only** thing that must be printed to
         STDOUT is:
           export CAPACITY_MODE="planner"
         (on its own line, followed by a trailing newline).

  Success criteria (what the automated grader checks):
    • /home/user/.bashrc exists.
    • The very last line in /home/user/.bashrc is exactly  
      export CAPACITY_MODE="planner"
      (there must be a trailing newline after it).
    • The agent’s final console output is a single line that matches the string
      export CAPACITY_MODE="planner"
      with no leading/trailing characters or blank lines.

  Notes:
    • You do NOT need root privileges.
    • Do not remove or alter any pre-existing content in /home/user/.bashrc.
    • Preserve file permissions (they should stay as the default user-writable
      settings).
truth:
Initial state (before the agent starts):
    • /home/user/.bashrc may or may not exist; if it exists, it does NOT contain
      the target line “export CAPACITY_MODE="planner"”.
    • No other files or directories are relevant.

  Expected final state:
    • /home/user/.bashrc exists and its very last line is:
        export CAPACITY_MODE="planner"\n
      (where “\n” represents a single newline character).
    • Any previous lines that were in /home/user/.bashrc stay untouched and
      appear before the new line.
    • File permissions on /home/user/.bashrc remain user-read/write
      (0600 or 0644 are both acceptable).

  Grader actions:
    1. Run: tail -n 1 /home/user/.bashrc  
       -> must output the literal string  
          export CAPACITY_MODE="planner"
    2. Verify that the file ends with a newline (using “od -An -c” or similar).
